questions.question: Explain the difference between direct and indirect addressing modes with examples.
expectedPoints: Direct addressing: operand address is directly specified in instruction
Indirect addressing: instruction contains address of a pointer to the operand
Example: LOAD R1, 1000 (direct) vs LOAD R1, (1000) (indirect)
Memory access patterns differ: 1 reference vs 2 references

questions.question: Compare and contrast the addressing modes typically found in RISC and CISC architectures, discussing their impact on instruction set design and performance.
expectedPoints: CISC: Many complex addressing modes, memory-to-memory operations
RISC: Few simple addressing modes, load-store architecture
Impact on instruction length: CISC variable-length, RISC fixed-length
Performance implications: CISC powerful but complex, RISC simpler but may need more instructions
Hardware complexity: CISC requires more complex control unit
Code density: CISC typically more compact code
